title: franseria canescens
intro: Franseria canescens, also known as the gray rabbitbrush, is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Franseria canescens include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antimicrobial agent to treat wounds and infections. Franseria canescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in poultices and salves to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
